title: bitwise on sized ints only
manager: Bitwise operators (and/or/xor/not, shifts) restricted to sized integers only — because bit-twiddling on a 53-bit float-backed int is exactly where silent corruption lives. By requiring a declared width, FSL makes bit manipulation mean something precise, and refuses it on the types where it wouldn't. If you want to shift bits, you first have to say how many bits there are.
cs: bitwise ops only on sized ints (W6.2); shifts respect the declared width; a type error on unsized/float operands.
